name: Corn Crepes
recipeInstructions:
1. In a bowl, beat together the eggs, milk, water, and salt until well combined.
2. Gradually add the flour and masa harina, stirring continuously to avoid lumps. Stir in the melted butter until smooth.
- Alternatively, place all ingredients in a blender and blend until perfectly smooth, scraping down the sides as needed.
3. Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and let the batter rest for at least an hour (or longer) to allow flavors to meld and make it easier to work with.
4. Heat a non-stick crepe pan over medium heat and brush lightly with butter.
5. Pour just enough batter to cover the bottom of the pan, tilting the pan to spread evenly—about 1/4 cup.
6. Cook for about one minute until the bottom is golden brown, then gently peel up the edges using a spatula.
7. Gently flip the crepe and cook the other side until golden and speckled with brown spots (less than a minute).
8. Repeat steps 4-7 with remaining batter.
9. Stack cooked crepes on a platter and cover loosely with a slightly damp kitchen towel to prevent them from drying out.
10. The crepes can be made up to 24 hours in advance; store tightly covered with plastic wrap in the refrigerator.
11. Before using, allow chilled crepes to come to room temperature or warm briefly in a non-stick pan for a few seconds to soften before folding or rolling.
Makes about 18 to 20 crepes.
